author: Dr. Jennifer Adams
content:
TELEPHONE ENCOUNTER — Lab Results Review

Patient: Robert Morrison, 72M
Call initiated by: Office (RN callback per patient portal message)
Spoke with: Patient and wife Patricia

Reason for Call:
Mr. Morrison sent a portal message requesting review of his recent laboratory results drawn on 9/5/2024.

Lab Results Reviewed:
- BMP: Na 136, K 4.7, Cl 101, CO2 24, BUN 38, Cr 1.93 (eGFR 36.5), Glucose 142
- CBC: WBC 6.8, Hgb 11.0, Hct 33.1%, Plt 192
- HbA1c: 7.4% (improved from 7.8% in June — empagliflozin likely contributing)
- BNP: 420 (stable, slight improvement from 450 in June)
- Lipid panel: Total cholesterol 168, LDL 82, HDL 38, Triglycerides 198
- Hepatic panel: AST 30, ALT 34, ALP 88, T. bili 0.7
- UACR: 165 mg/g (A2, moderately increased, stable)

Discussion:
1. Kidney function stable at eGFR 36.5, essentially unchanged from June (36.5). Reassured patient that CKD is not accelerating. UACR 165, stable. Continue current renal protective regimen.

2. HbA1c improved to 7.4% from 7.8% — best result in over a year. Empagliflozin appears to be providing significant benefit for glycemic control in addition to cardiac and renal protection. Patient pleased with this result. Encouraged continued dietary adherence.

3. Hemoglobin 11.0, stable. CKD-related anemia, not worsening. Not yet at threshold for EPO-stimulating agents.

4. BNP 420, slightly improved from June baseline. Heart failure stable on current GDMT.

5. Potassium 4.7, acceptable but needs continued monitoring given Entresto + spironolactone + empagliflozin. Reminded patient to avoid high-potassium foods in excess (bananas, oranges, potatoes).

6. Triglycerides 198 (elevated), HDL 38 (low). Discussed lifestyle modifications. Already on atorvastatin 80mg. Triglycerides likely related to diabetes and metabolic syndrome. Will monitor.

7. Patient asked about starting exercise — encouraged walking as tolerated, starting with 10-15 minutes daily and gradually increasing. Must wear supportive shoes given neuropathy. Avoid exercise in extreme heat or cold.

8. Wife asked about the empagliflozin and UTI risk — reassured that patient has been on it since May without genital infections or UTIs. Continue good hygiene practices and adequate hydration within fluid restriction.

Plan:
- Continue all current medications
- Repeat labs (BMP, CBC, A1c) in 3 months (December 2024)
- Nephrology follow-up in 3 months
- Cardiology follow-up as scheduled
- PCP visit in 6-8 weeks for comprehensive follow-up
- Patient reminded to call if weight increases >3 lbs/day, worsening swelling, or new symptoms

Patient and wife verbalized understanding. No additional questions.
attestation:
Telephone encounter documented. Lab results reviewed and discussed with patient and wife.

Electronically signed by: Dr. Jennifer Adams, MD
Date: 9/8/2024
